project_type: Python Telegram bot using python-telegram-bot async (Application class) with OMR scanning via OpenCV
build_system: pip with requirements.txt, deployed via systemd on Ubuntu
test_framework: No existing tests - validation is through syntax checking and import verification
test_command: python -c "import test_grader_bot.bot; import test_grader_bot.omr_scanner; import test_grader_bot.sheet_generator; import test_grader_bot.excel_export; import test_grader_bot.config; print('All imports OK')"
verification_instructions: 1. Install dependencies with pip. 2. Verify all modules import without errors. 3. Check Python syntax with py_compile on all .py files. 4. Verify no circular imports. 5. Run basic unit tests if created.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET - full network access. Python 3.11 default. No .env file present (bot won't actually run without BOT_TOKEN), but code should be importable and syntactically valid.
contribution_requirements: All user-facing text in Uzbek. Keep under 120MB RAM. No new heavy dependencies. JSON file storage. Python 3.10+ compatible.

key_patterns.bot_architecture: python-telegram-bot v20+ async Application with ConversationHandler. States defined as integer constants. Single ADMIN_ID access control.
key_patterns.session_storage: JSON files in data/ directory. _load_session/_save_session pattern using user_id-keyed files.
key_patterns.omr_approach: OpenCV: detect 4 corner markers -> perspective transform to fixed SHEET_WIDTH/SHEET_HEIGHT -> analyze bubble fill ratios with Otsu threshold per ROI
key_patterns.sheet_layout_constants: All dimensions defined in sheet_generator.py and imported by omr_scanner.py: SHEET_WIDTH=2480, SHEET_HEIGHT=3508, BUBBLE_RADIUS=22, BUBBLE_SPACING_X=90, BUBBLE_SPACING_Y=80, etc.
key_patterns.student_numbers: Two-row bubble section for tens (0-4) and units (0-9) digits, allowing numbers 1-50

directory_structure: test_grader_bot/ - flat module structure: bot.py (main), config.py (settings), omr_scanner.py (CV processing), sheet_generator.py (PDF/image generation), excel_export.py (xlsx output), data/ (JSON session files)
